zh.Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Brit: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: William Fry是Mr. Fry和Mrs. Fry的兒子，於大約1606年出生在英國。他於1638年與Elizabeth Foster在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1642年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯享年37歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Fry是Mr. Fry和Mrs. Fry的儿子，于大约1606年出生在英国。他于1638年与Elizabeth Foster在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1642年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯享年37岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Fry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southern and southwestern): from Middle English frie ‘free’ (Old English frīo, frīg), with reference either to rank or tenurial status (‘free-born’) or to character (‘generous, open-handed’). It is synonymous with Free .
Americanized form of German Frei or Frey , or of the Dutch cognate Vrij.

variants.name: Frye
meanings: History: Joseph Frye (1711/12–94) was a military officer from Andover, MA, where the family had long been of local prominence. In 1762, he was granted a township in ME, later named Fryeburg after him, and moved his family there. His great-great-grandson William Pierce Frye was born in Lewiston, ME, and served in Congress, first as a member of the House of Representatives and then the Senate from 1871 until his death in 1911.

variants.name: Free
meanings: English: nickname or status name from Middle English fre ‘free’ (Old English frēo), with reference to either tenurial status (‘free born’) or to character (‘noble, generous, open-handed’).
Americanized form of Swiss German and German   Frei   and   Frey  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Fry was born about 1606, in England as the son of Mr. Fry and Mrs. Fry. He married Elizabeth Foster in 1638, in Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died in 1642, in Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 37, and was buried in Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: William Fryは約1606年にイギリスでMr. FryとMrs. Fryの息子として生まれた。1638年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーウエイモウスでElizabeth Fosterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1642年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーウエイモウスで37歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクウエイモウスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Fry(은)는 대략 1606년 영국에서 Mr. Fry(와)과 Mrs. Fry의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1638년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 w이모우스에서 Elizabeth Foster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1642년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 w이모우스에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 w이모우스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Fry nasceu aproximadamente 1606, em Inglaterra filho de Mr. Fry e Mrs. Fry. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Foster em 1638, em Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1642, em Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: William Fry родился около 1606 г. в местности Англия. Его родителей звали Mr. Fry и Mrs. Fry. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Foster в 1638 г. в местности вимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер в 1642 г. в местности вимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности вимоут, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
